title: Replace static status bar with live scheduler counts
description: Update the TUI status bar to show live counts for running, ready, and blocked beads plus the scheduler auto/manual mode, replacing the current static summary string.
acceptance_criteria: `src/agent_takt/tui/app.py` renders status text in the form `N running | N ready | N blocked | S:auto/manual`.
The counts are derived from current bead state on each refresh and stay synchronized with scheduler mode state already tracked by the app.
The status bar change does not introduce an additional countdown or refresh-only timer display.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `BEAD_IN_PROGRESS` to the model imports. Added module-level `_live_status_bar_text(runtime_state)` that counts in_progress/ready/blocked beads from the current bead cache and formats the scheduler mode as `S:auto` (continuous_run_enabled=True) or `S:manual`. Modified `_update_status_panel()` to call this helper instead of `runtime_state.status_panel_text()`. The status_message is appended after the live counts so user feedback (e.g. "Selected B-xxx") is still visible.
handoff_summary.risks: Bead counts reflect the current filter mode. With FILTER_ACTIONABLE selected, in_progress beads are excluded from the filter, so `running` would display as 0 even if beads are executing. This is a known limitation consistent with how the existing footer_text behaves; FILTER_DEFAULT (the startup default) correctly includes in_progress beads.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester should verify: (1) status bar shows the correct counts for in_progress/ready/blocked beads with default filter, (2) S:auto appears when continuous_run_enabled, S:manual otherwise, (3) counts update when bead state changes via refresh, (4) status_message still appears appended after the live counts.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. Only `app.py` was modified; `state.py`'s `status_panel_text()` method is no longer called from `_update_status_panel()` but remains intact for any other callers.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Implemented `_live_status_bar_text` as a module-level function (not a method) so it can be tested independently of the Textual app. It takes a `TuiRuntimeState` argument, counts bead statuses from `runtime_state.beads` (the filter-respecting cache), and derives scheduler mode from `continuous_run_enabled`. The existing `status_panel_text()` in state.py was left untouched — only `_update_status_panel()` was changed to call the new helper. The status_message is appended after the required format rather than replacing it, preserving user-facing feedback messages.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Test: call `_live_status_bar_text` with a mocked TuiRuntimeState where beads include a mix of statuses, verify the output matches `N running | N ready | N blocked | S:manual`. Test with `continuous_run_enabled=True` to verify `S:auto`. Test that status_message is appended. Regression: ensure `_update_status_panel()` no longer calls `status_panel_text()` (which would include timer/mode text). If there are existing TUI tests that assert on status bar content, they will need updating to match the new format.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Bead counts are derived from the filtered bead cache, not from all beads globally. When `FILTER_ACTIONABLE` is active, `in_progress` beads are excluded and `running` will show 0. Fixing this cleanly would require either a separate all-beads cache in `TuiRuntimeState` (touching state.py, outside this bead's scope) or a separate storage load on each render (expensive). The default filter (FILTER_DEFAULT) correctly includes in_progress beads.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. Only `app.py` was modified; `state.py`'s `status_panel_text()` method is no longer called from `_update_status_panel()` but remains intact for any other callers.

execution_history.summary: Updated the TUI status bar to show live bead counts in the form `N running | N ready | N blocked | S:auto/manual` by adding a `_live_status_bar_text()` helper function in `app.py` and wiring it into `_update_status_panel()`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
